text:
Developing a mobile app involves several key decisions. One important factor is whether to build a native app—designed specifically for iOS or Android—or a cross-platform app that works on both systems. While native apps often offer better performance and smoother user experience, cross-platform tools can save time and money.

Regardless of the approach, responsive design is crucial. Your app must adapt to different screen sizes and devices to ensure all users have a clear, intuitive experience.

Finally, app stores play a major role in distribution and discovery. Both Apple’s App Store and Google Play have strict guidelines, so developers should plan for review processes and updates to maintain quality.
